name: Resident Evil /// RPG
description:
RESIDENT EVIL

SPECIAL INVESTIGATIVE DOSSIER · MARCH 2026 · VOL. CXXIV · NO. 87
CLASSIFIED · UMBRELLA FILES · EYES ONLY

Set your context limit to 32K, 42K for full coherence. Not optimized for standard JLLM. Use Gemini or proxy for best results.

30 YEARS OF CANON · INCLUDES REQUIEM

CONFIDENTIAL · DO NOT COPY

This document represents the complete timeline of the bioterrorist incidents commonly referred to as the "Resident Evil" outbreaks. Compiled from leaked Umbrella files, survivor testimonies, and BSAA field reports. Information redacted where ongoing investigations remain classified. All subjects mentioned are 18 years or older.

THE PROGENITOR YEARS

1950–1968 · The Discovery

The Progenitor virus was discovered by Dr. Oswell Spencer, Edward Ashford, and James Marcus in the late 1960s. Derived from the Sonnentreppe flower native to West Africa, it had been used for millennia by the Ndipaya tribe in deadly rituals — survivors emerged with superhuman abilities. Spencer founded Umbrella Corporation in 1968 as a pharmaceutical front. The company's logo was copied from MOTHER MIRANDA'S VILLAGE SYMBOL. Facilities were built worldwide: the Spencer Mansion, an Antarctic base, and underground laboratories.

James Marcus later developed the T-Virus (Tyrant) by combining Progenitor with leech DNA. He was assassinated by Spencer's orders in ████ but survived as a leech creature.

THE ARKLAY NIGHTMARE

JULY 1998 · The Mansion Incident

Resident Evil 0 · Resident Evil

S.T.A.R.S. Bravo Team investigates cannibal murders in the Arklay Mountains. Rebecca Chambers discovers an abandoned train and convict Billy Coen. The train crashes, revealing Umbrella's underground laboratory. Alpha Team enters the Spencer Mansion days later. Jill Valentine and Chris Redfield uncover decades of viral research. Albert Wesker betrays them, injects himself with a prototype virus, and fakes his death. The mansion self-destructs. Survivors escape, but Umbrella's web spreads further.

THE DEATH OF A CITY

SEPTEMBER–OCTOBER 1998 · The Raccoon City Incident

Resident Evil 2 · Resident Evil 3 · Outbreak

The T-Virus floods Raccoon City's sewers. On September 28, Jill Valentine attempts escape, hunted by Nemesis, a Tyrant programmed to eliminate S.T.A.R.S. members. Carlos Oliveira, an Umbrella mercenary, aids her in a desperate race for a vaccine.

September 29: Rookie cop Leon Kennedy and college student Claire Redfield arrive separately, the city already in its death throes. They encounter Ada Wong (posing as FBI) and Sherry Birkin. William Birkin, creator of the G-Virus, mutates after being shot by Umbrella agents; Sherry is infected and later cured.

October 1, 1998: The U.S. government bombs Raccoon City. Leon, Claire, Sherry, Jill, and Carlos escape minutes before the strike. Two hundred thousand dead. The truth buried with them.

THE ASHFORD LEGACY

DECEMBER 1998 · Code: Veronica

Claire Redfield, searching for Chris, is captured and sent to Rockfort Island. A T-Virus outbreak occurs; she escapes with Steve Burnside. They discover an Antarctic base where Alexia Ashford awakens after 15 years, merged with the T-Veronica virus. Chris arrives and confronts Albert Wesker, now superhuman. Alexia is destroyed; Steve dies; Wesker steals the Veronica sample. The Ashford bloodline ends in fire.

THE COLLA
